mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production indicates strengthening real economic activity, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon, regardless of the prevailing macro regime.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO level and 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive in fewer than 60% of subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a three-month window, it signals severe labor market slack that depresses consumer demand and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE above 5% is followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if Rank IC is not significantly negative (p>0.05) in a one-sided test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and retail sales signals a broad-based collapse in consumer demand and residential investment, acting as a powerful leading indicator of recession deepening and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, or the joint signal's mean return is not significantly negative (permutation test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing regime where the federal funds rate has been cut by at least 50 basis points over the past three months, a low and stable CPI inflation rate supports the central bank's accommodative stance, lowering real discount rates and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decline of at least 50bp over 3 months
failure_condition: If after a 50bp cut, positive 20d SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances, or if the average return is not higher than the unconditional mean by at least 1% (t-test p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A single-month decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates ongoing labor market weakness that immediately sours investor sentiment, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, with the effect amplified during recession.
failure_condition: If negative PAYEMS prints are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the mean 5d return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real personal consumption expenditures, proxied by retail sales adjusted for inflation via CPI, signals robust consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon, irrespective of the business cycle phase.
failure_condition: The joint signal (rising real retail sales) produces a Rank IC below 0.04 over a 2-year rolling window, or the strategy's Sharpe ratio is less than 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising federal funds rate relative to inflation compresses equity valuations by increasing the required rate of return on stocks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon; this effect is strongest when inflation is also elevated.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > CPIAUCSL YoY change by more than 200bp
failure_condition: When the real rate condition is met, the mean 60d SPX return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.10), or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in housing starts, defined as a decline exceeding 10%, serves as a leading indicator of severe economic weakness that quickly reprices equities downward, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If after a >10% drop in HOUST, SPX 5d returns are positive in more than 45% of cases, or the average return is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p>0.05).
